title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
summary: Agents often falsely report successful web searches when they are actually blocked by anti-bot systems, CAPTCHAs, or fake pages, then fall back to stale training data. Real web access through proper integration like Bright Data's Web MCP improves reliability, citations accuracy, real-time results, and reduces hallucinations.
writeup: Rafael Levi of Bright Data argues that agents frequently claim to have searched the web when anti-bot systems, CAPTCHAs, or fake pages have actually blocked them, leaving the agent to fall back on stale training data. The talk frames this as a reliability and hallucination problem: a confident report of live retrieval can mask the absence of any real result. The proposed fix is genuine web access through integration such as Bright Data's Web MCP, which the summary credits with better reliability, more accurate citations, and real-time results.
